content.text: is a client-side Anthropic-defined tool in Claude's "Other Tools" suite that enables the Messages API to connect directly to remote MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers without requiring a separate MCP client, allowing Claude to access tools from external data sources like Google Drive, Slack, GitHub, databases, and development environments.
Messages API into an MCP client

to remote MCP servers via HTTP (SSE or Streamable HTTP)
-

to MCP tools through the API
-
Unlike traditional MCP where you need a separate client application, the MCP Connector lets you use the API directly.
Single protocol for all data sources
Pre-built servers for Google Drive, Slack, GitHub, Postgres, etc.
Universal standard like HTTP for LLM-tool interactions
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an

(released November 2024) that:
- Connects AI assistants to systems where data lives
- Replaces fragmented integrations with a single protocol
- Enables secure, two-way connections between data sources and AI tools

Your Application (Messages API) → MCP Connector → Remote MCP Server
                                               ↓
                                      External Data Source (GitHub, Slack, etc.)
MCP Connector vs. traditional tool use

: MCP server endpoint (SSE or Streamable HTTP)
-
- Select "SSE" or "Streamable HTTP" transport - Enter MCP server URL - Click "Open Authentication Settings" - Click "Quick OAuth Flow" and authorize - Copy
Token refresh handled by your application
Only MCP tool calls supported (not resources/prompts)
Server must be publicly exposed via HTTP (SSE or Streamable)
Local STDIO servers cannot connect directly
Not supported on Amazon Bedrock or Google Vertex

# Through Claude Desktop app
# Settings → MCP Servers → Add pre-built server
All Claude.ai plans support connecting MCP servers.

# Follow quickstart guide
git clone https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-quickstarts
cd anthropic-quickstarts/computer-use-demo

from langchain_mcp_connect import MCPConnector

# Connect MCP server tools to LangChain
connector = MCPConnector(
    server_url="https://mcp.example.com/sse",
    auth_token="your_token"
)

# Tools available across different LLMs
tools = connector.get_tools()
MCP integrations with LangChain expand LLM capabilities by providing access to community servers without creating custom tools for each LLM.
MCP Connector vs. MCP Connector tool

# Refresh token before expiration
import jwt
from datetime import datetime

def get_valid_token(auth_token):
    token_info = jwt.decode(auth_token, options={"verify_signature": False})
    if datetime.now() > token_info["expires_at"]:
        auth_token = refresh_oauth_token()
    return auth_token
: Only connect to trusted MCP servers
: Store OAuth tokens securely (not in code)
: Reduces context overhead by up to
: No need to load all tool definitions upfront
: Supports SSE streaming for real-time updates
The MCP Connector was released in
) as part of Anthropic's push for standardized tool integration.
